# Example Output Structure

This folder demonstrates how Swark 2.0 organizes architecture diagrams according to your handwritten specification.

## Naming Convention

Files follow this pattern:
```
YYYY-MM-DD__HH-MM-SS__commit-<hash>__<level>-<format>-diagram.<extension>
```

## Example Files (will be generated by the extension):

### Commit Analysis Results
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__high-level-d2-diagram.md`
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__high-level-eraser-diagram.yaml`
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__semi-d2-diagram.md`
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__semi-eraser-diagram.yaml`
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__detailed-d2-diagram.md`
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__detailed-eraser-diagram.yaml`
- `2025-08-29__14-30-25__commit-abc123__high-level-log.md`

### Current Directory Analysis
- `2025-08-29__14-35-10__all-three-d2-diagram.md`
- `2025-08-29__14-35-10__all-three-eraser-diagram.yaml`
- `2025-08-29__14-35-10__all-three-log.md`

This structure allows you to:
1. Track architectural changes over time
2. Compare different commits
3. Organize by diagram detail level
4. Support both D2 and Eraser formats
5. Maintain chronological order
